children.heading: Appointment and compensation
children.label: a Appointment and compensation
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to appoint and fix the compensation of such officers and employees, including attorneys, as may be necessary to carry out the functions of the Secretary and the Department. Except as otherwise provided by law, such officers and employees shall be appointed in accordance with the civil service laws and their compensation fixed in accordance with title 5.

children.heading: Applicability of General Schedule; termination of authority; exemption from limitations on executive positions
children.label: b Applicability of General Schedule; termination of authority; exemption from limitations on executive positions

children.text: At the request of the Secretary, the Director of the Office of Personnel Management shall, under , provide for the establishment in each of the grade levels GS–16, GS–17, and GS–18 of a number of positions in the Department equal to the number of positions in that grade level which were used primarily for the performance of functions and offices transferred under this chapter and which were assigned and filled on the day before .section 5108 of title 5May 4, 1980

Appointments to positions provided for under this subsection may be made without regard to the provisions of , if the individual appointed in such position is an individual who is transferred in connection with the transfer of functions and offices under this chapter and, on the day preceding , holds a position and has duties comparable to those of the position to which appointed hereunder.section 3324 of title 5May 4, 1980
The authority under this subsection with respect to any position shall terminate when the person first appointed to fill such position ceases to hold such position.

For purposes of section 414(a)(3)(A) of the Civil Service Reform Act of 1978, an individual appointed under this subsection shall be deemed to occupy the same position as the individual occupied on the day preceding .May 4, 1980

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 99–498, title XIV, § 1401(d)100 Stat. 1597Oct. 17, 1986
children.label: c Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 99–498, title XIV, § 1401(d)100 Stat. 1597Oct. 17, 1986

children.heading: Senior Executive Service
children.label: d Senior Executive Service
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Director of the Office of Personnel Management shall establish positions within the Senior Executive Service for 15 limited-term appointees. The Secretary shall appoint individuals to such positions as provided by . Such positions shall expire on the later of three years after , or three years after the initial appointment to each position. Positions in effect under this subsection shall be taken into account in applying the limitations on positions prescribed under section 3134(e) and section 5108 of such title.section 3394 of title 5May 4, 1980

children.heading: Indian preference laws
children.label: e Indian preference laws
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to prevent the application of any Indian preference law in effect on the day before , to any function or office transferred by this chapter and subject to any such law on the day before . Any function or office transferred by this chapter and subject to any such law shall continue to be subject to any such law.October 17, 1979October 17, 1979
